rows.notes: Page title centered at top. Handwriting clear and unambiguous; no monetary columns on this line.

Pounds read as '30' (two-digit) in leftmost of the three columns; shillings '14' and pence '10' are clearly aligned under shilling/pence ruled columns. Slight smudge on the pounds digit makes it slightly uncertain but alignment supports '30'.
Numerals '6' / '3' / '10' are well formed and sit squarely in the three currency columns; all values within valid ranges.
Clear three-column reading: pounds '25', shillings '12', pence '5'. Handwriting is distinct and columns unambiguous.
Pounds look like a single-digit '9' but slightly smudged; shillings '11' and pence '5' legible. Slight uncertainty on the pounds digit due to ink thickness.

Left column appears to contain two digits ('8' and '4') but the ink of the left-most digit bleeds into the margin making it ambiguous whether it is '84' or '8 4'. Shillings '12' and pence '7' are legible. Chosen '84' based on width and alignment with other two-digit pounds on page but confidence moderate.
Clear small numerals '5 / 1 / 5' in the three currency columns; inscription and numbers legible.
Second line with identical '5 / 1 / 5' pattern; handwriting and alignment match the previous row, clear reading.

Pounds '23' and shillings '18' are clear and sit properly in the pounds/shillings columns; pence looks like small '1'. There is a faint scribble adjacent (possible annotation) but core numbers are legible.

Shillings '15' and pence '3' are readable; the pounds digit is a small '3' but there are additional stray marks to the right of the pence that could be marginal notes. Chosen '3' for pounds; moderate confidence due to extra ink nearby.
Aligned values read as '9 / 8 / 7'. Description legible. Slightly slanted ink but digits distinct.

Pounds '3' is clear; shillings look like '19' (the '9' curls under), pence appears '2' though slightly faint. All in valid ranges; moderate confidence because the shilling '19' digit curl is similar to other marks.

rows.description: Yardington Goun. pro Anno at Porto Anni
rows.notes: Pounds '15' visible though the '1' is narrow; shillings '3' and pence '4' readable but pence slightly smudged. Context (similar layout) supports this reading.
Clear '10 / 0 / 0' in columns; no ambiguity.
Small pounds digit '1' then '10' shillings and '7' pence. The pounds digit is faint and could be misread as a mark; chosen '1' based on alignment and spacing. Moderate confidence.

rows.description: Rest Hawtin, pro Anno at Zin.
rows.notes: Two-digit pounds '88' clear and well aligned; shillings and pence both '0'. Ink darker here and digits distinct.
Large three-digit pounds '215' clearly written; shilling and pence zeros aligned. High confidence.
Clear '40 / 0 / 0' grouped in the currency columns; handwriting dark and legible.

rows.description: Shrupe, Bush, pro Anno at Zin ad Mich
rows.notes: Digits read as '7 / 10 / 0' with clear separation; description is moderately legible though abbreviated.
Clear '18 / 0 / 0' in the three columns; handwriting neat here.
Small '1' in pounds column, zeros for shillings and pence. Pounds digit is narrow but alignment supports reading.
Pounds column blank/zero; shillings read '2' and pence '6'. Ink faint for pounds but shillings/pence are aligned and legible.
Pounds clearly zero/blank. Shillings look like '13' but the final digit curl is slightly ambiguous; pence looks like '6' but faint. Moderate confidence.
Another low-value line with pounds blank. Shillings '2' and pence '6' similar to an earlier line; faint ink increases uncertainty.
Digits read as '1 / 13 / 4' though the '1' is small; spacing consistent with currency columns. Moderate confidence.

rows.description: Hart Hall pro Anno ad Mich.
rows.notes: Clear numerals '9 / 6 / 8' in their respective columns; description legible. Good alignment.

rows.description: Ladslane Comyn, pro Anno ad Mich
rows.notes: Read as '8 / 6 / 8' with neat digits; proper column alignment. Some letters in name unclear but amounts clear.
Clearly '5 / 0 / 0' in currency columns; name abbreviated but recognizable as 'do' (ditto).

rows.description: Modingham pro tribus Paribus anni ad Mich
rows.notes: Clear '10 / 0 / 0' with strong dark digits and good alignment.
Numerals '8 / 1 / 4' clear; letters in description slightly compressed but readable.

rows.description: Wilmot Gos pro Quadrinum ad mic.
rows.notes: Pounds blank/zero; shillings clearly '10', pence '0'. Alignment consistent with other rows.
Numbers read as '6 / 13 / 8' with clear separation; pence '8' distinct. Good column alignment.

rows.description: Quit Rents of Little Gow pro Biorn ad Mich.
rows.notes: This line is faint and partially obscured by a small ink blot near the pence; the left-most pounds digit appears to be '1', shillings maybe '1', pence possibly '2' but ink bleed and cramped writing make reading uncertain. Chosen the simplest aligned reading but confidence low.

rows.description: Lord myer Barret pro Biorn. ad mic.
rows.notes: Pounds/read as '8', shillings '8', pence '4'. There are small annotations after the pence (looks like 'd g' or similar) that slightly obscure the final digit; moderate confidence.

rows.description: Lord olin Bigg pro Biorn. ad mic.
rows.notes: Digits appear as '6 / 7 / 3' but there is an over-writing or ink flourish on the pence making the last digit slightly ambiguous (could be '3' or '6'). Chosen '3' based on shape and alignment with other rows.

rows.description: Burdon olin Calcott pro Biorn. ad mic.
rows.notes: Clear '9 / 0 / 0' values in the currency columns; description slightly cramped but legible.

rows.description: Backgate Emmes, pro Anno ad mic.
rows.notes: Pounds read '16', shillings '5', pence '0'. Good spacing and dark ink; clear reading.

rows.description: Wild Arish Willicomb pro Trin. at 4 parts An. ad mic.
rows.notes: Values appear as '14 / 0 / 0'. The long description is slightly cramped; the pounds figure is clear but the handwriting could be read as '16' in some stylings — chosen '14' based on clearer shape of the '4'.

rows.description: Bond of Gooch to Dr. Edgcumbe
rows.notes: Neat entry with '14 / 0 / 0' in the currency columns; description fairly legible ('Edgcumbe' spelled in old hand).
Clear '4 / 0 / 0' in columns; description read as 'Kidlington Vie.' (abbreviated).
Clear '1 / 0 / 0' at bottom-right area. Description legible.
Last visible line reads '1 / 8 / 4' clearly; handwriting crisp and aligned with the three currency columns.
